data.title: Trade Desk Inc. reported strong quarterly performance, yet stock plummeted. Analysts skeptical about future growth, citing concerns about sustained long-term growth and third-quarter outlook. BofA Securities downgraded rating from Buy to Underperform, slashing price target from $130 to $55. Deceleration in growth raising concerns among investors.
data.description: Trade Desk Inc. reported strong second-quarter revenues but its stock plummeted as investors remained unimpressed. Analysts, including Jessica Reif Ehrlich from BofA Securities, downgraded TTD's rating to Underperform and slashed the price target from $130 to $55. Concerns were raised about Trade Desk's ability to sustain long-term growth, as the company's third-quarter outlook indicated a deceleration in growth. Ehrlich mentioned limited visibility into the timing and magnitude of potential long-term growth drivers for TTD. Despite these concerns, there are still optimistic projections about Trade Desk's future growth prospects.

data.title: Bitcoin surged to $117,000 as Trump's crypto-friendly move impacts market; questions on sustainability remain.
data.description: Bitcoin has seen a significant rebound, reaching the $117,000 level, sparking trader interest. This surge is attributed to recent U.S. policy changes allowing 401(k) investment in cryptocurrencies and speculation on government debt monetization. Despite this, analysts at 10x Research believe the rally lacks a strong macro catalyst for a sustained breakout. The cryptocurrency hit a low of $111,643 on August 7 but then gained momentum. Factors contributing to the rally include Trump's directive for alternative assets in 401(k) accounts, the nomination of crypto-friendly Stephen Miran to the Fed board, and a 39% tax on 1kg of gold. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.
Trump, Putin To Meet End Of Next Week, Details Not Set - CNBC

data.title: Playback available for webinar on how risky driving behavior is affecting auto insurers. Drivers more at risk due to distractions and poor behaviors. Sponsored by Nationwide.
data.description: In a recent webinar hosted by AM Best and sponsored by Nationwide, titled “How Increasingly Risky Driving Behavior Is Impacting Auto Insurers,” video playback is now accessible. The webinar discusses the growing concern of poor driving behaviors and distractions like cell phone use affecting drivers. This heightened risk applies to all drivers, including personal vehicle operators and commercial fleet drivers. The discussion emphasizes the need for increased awareness and proactive measures to address these risks. With the webinar spanning one hour, a panel of insurance experts provide valuable insights on this pressing issue.

data.title: BerryDunn presents at 2025 MESC Conference after 25 years, showcasing expertise in Medicaid and HHS policy, with 4 days of events in WI.
data.description: BerryDunn, a comprehensive accounting, tax, and consulting firm, will attend the Medicaid Enterprise Systems Conference in Milwaukee, WI for the 25th consecutive year from August 11 to 14. The conference is a significant event for Medicaid and other Health and Human Services (HHS) sectors, focusing on policy, program proficiency, and healthcare data analytics. data.description: In Dubai, United Arab Emirates, the Dubai Electricity and Water Authority PJSC has announced a groundbreaking revenue of AED 14.6 billion for the initial six months of 2025.

data.title: SEC closes XRP case, ETH nears $4K, Gold hits ATH. Trump allows crypto in 401ks. XRP up 10%, ETH targets $4K. Miran joins Fed. $5B for ETH fund. 87% chance of ETH hitting $4K. XRP to acquire Rail for $200M. BTC mining stocks strong. ETH Foundation supports Storm’s defense. Animoca, Provenance launch RWA tokens. Binance, BBVA offer off-exchange custody. Winklevoss twins back American Bitcoin. Chainlink reveals LINK reserve. Tokenisation stalled at $25B. Pre-IPO stocks tradable on SOL.
data.description: President Trump has signed an executive order allowing cryptocurrency investment in 401ks. XRP saw a 10% increase following the SEC's official end to a legal battle. Ethereum is outperforming Bitcoin and is approaching the $4,000 resistance level. Miran, a crypto-friendly entity, is poised to join the Federal Reserve board. Fundamental Global is set to raise $5 billion for acquiring Ethereum. There is an 87% probability that Ethereum will reach $4,000 this month. XRP intends to purchase the payment platform Rail for $200 million. Bitcoin mining stocks are strong as concerns over tariffs diminish. Ethereum Foundation committed $500,000 for Storm’s legal battle. Animoca and Provenance have launched a tokenised Real-world asset market. An Animoca joint venture is seeking a stablecoin license. Binance and BBVA will offer off-exchange custody of assets. The Winklevoss twins have made an investment in Trump’s American Bitcoin. Chainlink has introduced the LINK reserve. JP Morgan reports that tokenization efforts are stalling at $25 billion. Pre-IPO stocks are now tradable on SOL.

data.title: MiniLuxe appoints Lanchi Venator as CFO and issues equity-based compensation to executives for shareholder alignment.
data.description: A highly experienced executive with a strong background in the beauty and multi-unit franchising sectors has joined MiniLuxe's team. The company continues to prioritize its shareholder-aligned equity-based incentive program for compensating executives and board members. This move comes as MiniLuxe looks to further enhance its leadership team with expertise in these areas.

data.description: SOGECLAIR, a business corporation operating with a capital of 3,204,901 Euros, is headquartered at 7 avenue Albert Durand in BLAGNAC, France. They can be contacted via telephone at +33 (0)5.61.71.71.71 or through their website www.sogeclair.com. The company is registered under the RCS TOULOUSE with the number 335 218 269.

data.title: Goodyear Tire faces challenges due to tariffs imposed by Trump, leading to a loss of 17 cents/share in Q2 earnings.
data.description: Goodyear Tire & Rubber Co (NASDAQ: GT) is among the companies affected by tariffs implemented by President Donald Trump. During its second-quarter earnings call, the company announced a loss of 17 cents per share, contrasting analyst expectations of a profit of 7 cents per share. Revenue stood at $4.46 billion, slightly below the anticipated $4.47 billion. CEO Mark Stewart attributed these challenges to disruptions in both consumer and commercial sectors due to shifts in global trade patterns, with a notable influx of low-cost imports impacting key markets. The term "tariff" was briefly mentioned in forward-looking statements. Investors seeking further insights on the impact of tariffs on Goodyear Tire should review the company's earnings report.

data.title: Hapbee launches Gift-A-Friend and Referral Programs led by Jaylen Brown; promotes wellness sharing and rewards in app.
data.description: Crafted by Jaylen Brown, Hapbee's Chief Innovation Officer and NBA Finals MVP, the Gift-A-Friend and Referral Programs aim to enhance the spirit of generosity. These new features allow members to exchange wellness benefits and receive rewards seamlessly through the Hapbee app.

data.title: Tevogen reports significant increase in institutional ownership, with 73% of holders increasing positions.
data.description: On August 8, 2025, Tevogen Bio Holdings Inc. (Nasdaq: TVGN) based in Warren, N.J., provided new information regarding institutional ownership of its common stock. This update was derived from data reported by institutional investment managers in their Form 13F submission to the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). The specifics of the institutional ownership were disclosed by the company.

data.title: Doximity (DOCS) beats Q1 earnings, reports 36 cents per share vs 30 cents estimate. Sales at $145.913M vs $139.705M estimate. FY2026 sales guidance raised to $628M-$636M. CEO cites strong profit growth and record engagement.
data.description: Healthcare technology company Doximity Inc (NYSE: DOCS) exceeded expectations with its first-quarter earnings report. The company's earnings of 36 cents per share surpassed the analyst consensus of 30 cents per share. Additionally, quarterly sales reached $145.913 million, beating the estimated $139.705 million. As a result, Doximity raised its FY2026 sales guidance from $619.000 million-$631.000 million to $628.000 million-$636.000 million. CEO Jeff Tangney highlighted the strong profit growth and increased engagement across their various products as key factors driving the company's success. For more details, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.
